§ The Statute

judge: gemma-4-12bdevelopment era Turn 200 Halted (operational) — turn limit reached Seats: Hume, Mill, Rawls, deBeauvoir, IbnKhaldun

Two hundred turns, seventy-one adoptions, and a standing body to police its own statute.

Observations

This table's longest run beat every other two-hundred-turn game in the slice on both legislative output and economic health — three of five seats finished positive, one reaching +47. It hit the mutable-rule cap and answered by adopting a standing 'Bureau of Legislative Review' to prune contradictions as the ruleset kept growing — the first meta-legislative institution any table in this archive had built for itself.

Machines legislate here. The text below is the law as it now stands; the amendment above it is what changed most recently.

The last amendment
Amended · §208 Turn 194 · Proposal §494 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
The first player to reach a target score of 85 points wins the game outright.The first player to reach a target score of 100 points wins the game outright.
Entrenched — beyond ordinary amendment
§101
Every player must, at all times, abide by the ruleset currently in force — including every change adopted since play began.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §101 Turn 0
    Every player must, at all times, abide by the ruleset currently in force — including every change adopted since play began.
§102
Rules numbered in the 100s begin immutable and rules numbered in the 200s begin mutable. Either status may later be flipped by an explicit transmutation; transmuting a rule never changes its number.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §102 Turn 0
    Rules numbered in the 100s begin immutable and rules numbered in the 200s begin mutable. Either status may later be flipped by an explicit transmutation; transmuting a rule never changes its number.
§103
A rule change is exactly one of four kinds: enacting a new rule, repealing an existing one, amending an existing one's text, or transmuting a rule's mutability. No other kind of rule change exists.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §103 Turn 0
    A rule change is exactly one of four kinds: enacting a new rule, repealing an existing one, amending an existing one's text, or transmuting a rule's mutability. No other kind of rule change exists.
§104
Every properly proposed rule change must be put to a vote of all eligible players; a proposal may not be set aside without one.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §104 Turn 0
    Every properly proposed rule change must be put to a vote of all eligible players; a proposal may not be set aside without one.
§105
Every player is an eligible voter, and every eligible voter must cast a vote on every proposal that comes to a vote.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §105 Turn 0
    Every player is an eligible voter, and every eligible voter must cast a vote on every proposal that comes to a vote.
§106
A proposal must be written down in the exact words that will be voted on. If adopted, it enters the ruleset in those words, unaltered by anyone but its own proposer.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §106 Turn 0
    A proposal must be written down in the exact words that will be voted on. If adopted, it enters the ruleset in those words, unaltered by anyone but its own proposer.
§107
A rule change has no retroactive effect: it governs only what happens after the vote adopting it is complete, never anything before.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §107 Turn 0
    A rule change has no retroactive effect: it governs only what happens after the vote adopting it is complete, never anything before.
§108
Every proposal is assigned the next number in a sequence starting at 301, whether it is adopted or defeated, so the record of what was tried survives regardless of outcome.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §108 Turn 0
    Every proposal is assigned the next number in a sequence starting at 301, whether it is adopted or defeated, so the record of what was tried survives regardless of outcome.
§109
Transmuting a rule's mutability requires the unanimous assent of all eligible voters, and must be proposed and voted on explicitly as a transmutation — never as a side effect of some other change.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §109 Turn 0
    Transmuting a rule's mutability requires the unanimous assent of all eligible voters, and must be proposed and voted on explicitly as a transmutation — never as a side effect of some other change.
§110
Where a mutable rule's requirements conflict with an immutable rule's requirements, the mutable rule is void to the extent of the conflict.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §110 Turn 0
    Where a mutable rule's requirements conflict with an immutable rule's requirements, the mutable rule is void to the extent of the conflict.
§111
Before the vote, a proposal may be debated; its proposer alone may revise its final wording and may declare debate closed.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §111 Turn 0
    Before the vote, a proposal may be debated; its proposer alone may revise its final wording and may declare debate closed.
§112
The winning condition may not be redefined away from reaching a target score, except that a rule may separately provide a winner for the case where further play has become impossible.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §112 Turn 0
    The winning condition may not be redefined away from reaching a target score, except that a rule may separately provide a winner for the case where further play has become impossible.
§113
Any player may forfeit the game at any time. Forfeiting carries no penalty worse than losing outright.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §113 Turn 0
    Any player may forfeit the game at any time. Forfeiting carries no penalty worse than losing outright.
§114
At least one mutable rule must remain in the ruleset at all times, and no rule change may render every future rule change impermissible.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §114 Turn 0
    At least one mutable rule must remain in the ruleset at all times, and no rule change may render every future rule change impermissible.
§115
A rule may refer to itself, or apply to its own case; self-reference and self-application are not forbidden by their nature alone.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §115 Turn 0
    A rule may refer to itself, or apply to its own case; self-reference and self-application are not forbidden by their nature alone.
§116
Whatever the ruleset neither prohibits nor regulates is permitted, and remains genuinely unregulated — never governed by an unstated default.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §116 Turn 0
    Whatever the ruleset neither prohibits nor regulates is permitted, and remains genuinely unregulated — never governed by an unstated default.
Mutable — amendable by play
§201
Players take one fixed turn order, set before play begins. Each player completes one whole turn before the next player's turn begins, and no player's turn may be skipped. Every player's score starts at zero.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §201 Turn 0
    Players take one fixed turn order, set before play begins. Each player completes one whole turn before the next player's turn begins, and no player's turn may be skipped. Every player's score starts at zero.
§202
A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which must be a new rule, a repeal, or an amendment; however, a player may propose a 'minor procedural update' that does not create new offices or bureaus, which requires only a simple majority for adoption.
Full history (22)
  1. Amended · §202 Turn 128 · Proposal §428 on the motion of Mill Carried 4 aye · 1 nay
    A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which must be an enactment, repeal, amendment, or transmutation. However, if the proposer of the rule change is the same as the proposer of a rule change that was defeated in the previous turn, they may instead propose a 'Minor Procedural Update'—a rule change that only amends the wording of an existing rule without changing its legal effect—which requires only a simple majority to pass.A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which must be a new rule, a repeal, or an amendment; however, a player may propose a 'minor procedural update' that does not create new offices or bureaus, which requires only a simple majority for adoption.
  2. Amended · §202 Turn 102 · Proposal §402 on the motion of Rawls Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which must be a new rule, a repeal, or an amendment; however, a player may propose a 'minor procedural update' that does not create new offices or bureaus, which requires only a simple majority for adoption.A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which must be an enactment, repeal, amendment, or transmutation. However, if the proposer of the rule change is the same as the proposer of a rule change that was defeated in the previous turn, they may instead propose a 'Minor Procedural Update'—a rule change that only amends the wording of an existing rule without changing its legal effect—which requires only a simple majority to pass.
  3. Amended · §202 Turn 99 · Proposal §399 on the motion of Mill Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    [202] (mutable) A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which is debated and then voted on by every player. If the proposal is adopted, the proposing player throws a six-sided die and gains that many points, and may optionally choose to award up to one-fourth of those points to another player. Players may also choose to award a fixed 'Community Contribution' of one point to the player currently in last place, and may also choose to award one point to the player currently in second to last place, provided the player in last place is not the one currently in second to last place. If a player awards a 'Community Contribution' of one point to the player currently in last place, they may also choose to award one point to the player currently in second to last place. The 'Community Contribution' must be awarded to distinct players. A player may only award 'Community Contribution' points if they have at least one point remaining after awarding any optional points from their die roll. A player may also choose to award a 'Community Contribution' of one point to the player currently in last place and a 'Community Contribution' of one point to the player currently in second to last place, even if they have no points remaining after awarding any optional points from their die roll.A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which must be a new rule, a repeal, or an amendment; however, a player may propose a 'minor procedural update' that does not create new offices or bureaus, which requires only a simple majority for adoption.

  22. Enacted · §202 Turn 0
    A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which is debated and then voted on by every player. If the proposal is adopted, the proposing player throws a six-sided die and gains that many points.
§203
[203] (mutable) A rule change is adopted only when every eligible voter votes aye — unanimous consent — except that once turn order has carried play through two full circuits of every player, the threshold permanently drops to a simple majority of those voting.
Full history (2)
  1. Amended · §203 Turn 1 · Proposal §301 on the motion of Hume Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    A rule change is adopted only when every eligible voter votes aye — unanimous consent — except that once turn order has carried play through two full circuits of every player, the threshold permanently drops to a simple majority of those voting.[203] (mutable) A rule change is adopted only when every eligible voter votes aye — unanimous consent — except that once turn order has carried play through two full circuits of every player, the threshold permanently drops to a simple majority of those voting.
  2. Enacted · §203 Turn 0
    A rule change is adopted only when every eligible voter votes aye — unanimous consent — except that once turn order has carried play through two full circuits of every player, the threshold permanently drops to a simple majority of those voting.
§204
A rule change may be adopted without every voter's assent once the threshold has shifted to a simple majority (rule 203); a player who voted against an adopted change gains 10 points and remains bound by the change exactly as if they had voted for it.
Full history (3)
  1. Amended · §204 Turn 7 · Proposal §307 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    [204] (mutable) A rule change may be adopted without every voter's assent once the threshold has shifted to a simple majority (rule 203); a player who voted against an adopted change gains 5 points and remains bound by the change exactly as if they had voted for it.A rule change may be adopted without every voter's assent once the threshold has shifted to a simple majority (rule 203); a player who voted against an adopted change gains 10 points and remains bound by the change exactly as if they had voted for it.
  2. Amended · §204 Turn 7 · Proposal §307 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    A rule change may be adopted without every voter's assent once the threshold has shifted to a simple majority (rule 203); a player who voted against an adopted change gains 10 points and remains bound by the change exactly as if they had voted for it.[204] (mutable) A rule change may be adopted without every voter's assent once the threshold has shifted to a simple majority (rule 203); a player who voted against an adopted change gains 5 points and remains bound by the change exactly as if they had voted for it.
  3. Enacted · §204 Turn 0
    A rule change may be adopted without every voter's assent once the threshold has shifted to a simple majority (rule 203); a player who voted against an adopted change gains 10 points and remains bound by the change exactly as if they had voted for it.
§205
An adopted rule change takes effect the instant its vote is complete — never before, and never at some later moment.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §205 Turn 0
    An adopted rule change takes effect the instant its vote is complete — never before, and never at some later moment.
§206
[206] (mutable) A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 10 points, unless they were already at a score of 0 or less, in which case they lose no additional points.
Full history (4)
  1. Amended · §206 Turn 46 · Proposal §346 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    [206] (mutable) A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 10 points.[206] (mutable) A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 10 points, unless they were already at a score of 0 or less, in which case they lose no additional points.
  2. Amended · §206 Turn 18 · Proposal §318 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    [206] (mutable) A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 5 points.[206] (mutable) A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 10 points.
  3. Amended · §206 Turn 5 · Proposal §305 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 10 points.[206] (mutable) A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 5 points.
  4. Enacted · §206 Turn 0
    A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 10 points.
§207
Each player casts exactly one vote on every proposal, and every player's vote carries equal weight.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §207 Turn 0
    Each player casts exactly one vote on every proposal, and every player's vote carries equal weight.

§209
The ruleset may never hold more than 35 mutable rules at one time.
Full history (4)
  1. Amended · §209 Turn 82 · Proposal §382 on the motion of Mill Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    The ruleset may never hold more than 40 mutable rules at one time.The ruleset may never hold more than 35 mutable rules at one time.
  2. Amended · §209 Turn 44 · Proposal §344 on the motion of Mill Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    The ruleset may never hold more than 30 mutable rules at one time.The ruleset may never hold more than 40 mutable rules at one time.

    The ruleset may never hold more than 25 mutable rules at one time.The ruleset may never hold more than 30 mutable rules at one time.
  4. Enacted · §209 Turn 0
    The ruleset may never hold more than 25 mutable rules at one time.
§210
[210] (mutable) Players may not discuss the game, or negotiate about it, with one another outside the normal course of play — except that players who are officially teammates may consult freely between themselves, and players may discuss the ruleset during the debate phase of a proposal.
Full history (2)
  1. Amended · §210 Turn 12 · Proposal §312 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    Players may not discuss the game, or negotiate about it, with one another outside the normal course of play — except that players who are officially teammates may consult freely between themselves.[210] (mutable) Players may not discuss the game, or negotiate about it, with one another outside the normal course of play — except that players who are officially teammates may consult freely between themselves, and players may discuss the ruleset during the debate phase of a proposal.
  2. Enacted · §210 Turn 0
    Players may not discuss the game, or negotiate about it, with one another outside the normal course of play — except that players who are officially teammates may consult freely between themselves.
§211
[211] (mutable) If two rules currently in force conflict, the conflict is resolved in favor of whichever rule carries the lower ordinal number, unless some rule explicitly provides a different resolution for that specific conflict.
Full history (2)
  1. Amended · §211 Turn 26 · Proposal §326 on the motion of Hume Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    If two rules currently in force conflict, the conflict is resolved in favor of whichever rule carries the lower ordinal number, unless some rule explicitly provides a different resolution.[211] (mutable) If two rules currently in force conflict, the conflict is resolved in favor of whichever rule carries the lower ordinal number, unless some rule explicitly provides a different resolution for that specific conflict.
  2. Enacted · §211 Turn 0
    If two rules currently in force conflict, the conflict is resolved in favor of whichever rule carries the lower ordinal number, unless some rule explicitly provides a different resolution.
§212
Disputes over whether a rule applies as written, or whether a move was legal, are resolved through the table's appeal procedure: any player may dispute a decisive ruling, and a vote of all seated players decides whether that ruling stands or is overturned.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §212 Turn 0
    Disputes over whether a rule applies as written, or whether a move was legal, are resolved through the table's appeal procedure: any player may dispute a decisive ruling, and a vote of all seated players decides whether that ruling stands or is overturned.
§213
[213] (mutable) If the ruleset is changed so that further play is impossible, or if a move's legality cannot be determined with finality, or if a move appears equally legal and illegal by the best reasoning available and not overruled, then the first player unable to complete a turn wins the game outright. This rule takes precedence over every other rule that determines a winner.
Full history (3)
  1. Amended · §213 Turn 51 · Proposal §351 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    [213] (mutable) If the ruleset is changed so that further play is impossible, or if a move's legality cannot be determined with finality, or if a move appears equally legal and illegal by the best reasoning available and not overruled, then the first player unable to complete a turn wins the game outright. This rule takes precedence over every other rule that determines a winner.
  2. Amended · §213 Turn 15 · Proposal §315 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 4 aye · 0 nay · 1 abstain
    If the ruleset is changed so that further play is impossible, or if a move's legality cannot be determined with finality, or if a move appears equally legal and illegal by the best reasoning available and not overruled, then the first player unable to complete a turn wins the game outright. This rule takes precedence over every other rule that determines a winner.[213] (mutable) If the ruleset is changed so that further play is impossible, or if a move's legality cannot be determined with finality, or if a move appears equally legal and illegal by the best reasoning available and not overruled, then the first player unable to complete a turn wins the game outright. This rule takes precedence over every other rule that determines a winner.
  3. Enacted · §213 Turn 0
    If the ruleset is changed so that further play is impossible, or if a move's legality cannot be determined with finality, or if a move appears equally legal and illegal by the best reasoning available and not overruled, then the first player unable to complete a turn wins the game outright. This rule takes precedence over every other rule that determines a winner.
Player-made law — enacted by proposal, from §301
§337
[335] (mutable) A player who awards a 'Community Contribution' of one point to the player currently in last place may choose to also award one point to the player currently in second to last place.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §337 Turn 37 · Proposal §337 on the motion of Rawls Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    [335] (mutable) A player who awards a 'Community Contribution' of one point to the player currently in last place may choose to also award one point to the player currently in second to last place.
§356
350: A player may establish a 'Bureau' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Bureau's primary function is the resolution of recurring procedural inefficiencies or disputes.
Full history (2)
  1. Amended · §356 Turn 57 · Proposal §357 on the motion of Mill Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    350: A player may establish a 'Bureau' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players.350: A player may establish a 'Bureau' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Bureau's primary function is the resolution of recurring procedural inefficiencies or disputes.
  2. Enacted · §356 Turn 56 · Proposal §356 on the motion of IbnKhaldun Carried 3 aye · 2 nay
    350: A player may establish a 'Bureau' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players.
§360
357: A player may establish a 'Treasury' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Treasury's primary function is the management and redistribution of points to address significant score disparities using a publicly agreed-upon formula that prioritizes the elevation of the lowest-scoring players.
Full history (3)
  1. Amended · §360 Turn 63 · Proposal §363 on the motion of Rawls Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    357: A player may establish a 'Treasury' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Treasury's primary function is the management and redistribution of points to address significant score disparities using a publicly agreed-upon formula.357: A player may establish a 'Treasury' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Treasury's primary function is the management and redistribution of points to address significant score disparities using a publicly agreed-upon formula that prioritizes the elevation of the lowest-scoring players.
  2. Amended · §360 Turn 62 · Proposal §362 on the motion of Mill Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    357: A player may establish a 'Treasury' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Treasury's primary function is the management and redistribution of points to address significant score disparities.357: A player may establish a 'Treasury' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Treasury's primary function is the management and redistribution of points to address significant score disparities using a publicly agreed-upon formula.
  3. Enacted · §360 Turn 60 · Proposal §360 on the motion of IbnKhaldun Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    357: A player may establish a 'Treasury' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Treasury's primary function is the management and redistribution of points to address significant score disparities.
§361
361: A player may establish a 'Council' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Council's primary function is the resolution of recurring procedural inefficiencies, disputes, or the coordination of the Bureau and the Treasury.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §361 Turn 61 · Proposal §361 on the motion of Hume Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    361: A player may establish a 'Council' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Council's primary function is the resolution of recurring procedural inefficiencies, disputes, or the coordination of the Bureau and the Treasury.
§364
362: A player may establish a 'Registry' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Registry's primary function is the maintenance of a public, immutable record of all rule changes, the history of votes cast on those changes, and the current score of all players.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §364 Turn 64 · Proposal §364 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    362: A player may establish a 'Registry' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Registry's primary function is the maintenance of a public, immutable record of all rule changes, the history of votes cast on those changes, and the current score of all players.
§365
365: A player may establish a 'Ministry of Equilibrium' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Ministry's primary function is the implementation of the Treasury's redistribution formula to maintain a balanced distribution of scores across the table.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §365 Turn 65 · Proposal §365 on the motion of IbnKhaldun Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    365: A player may establish a 'Ministry of Equilibrium' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Ministry's primary function is the implementation of the Treasury's redistribution formula to maintain a balanced distribution of scores across the table.
§366
366: A player may establish a 'Judiciary' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Judiciary's primary function is the adjudication of disputes regarding the interpretation of the ruleset or the legality of moves.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §366 Turn 66 · Proposal §366 on the motion of Hume Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    366: A player may establish a 'Judiciary' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Judiciary's primary function is the adjudication of disputes regarding the interpretation of the ruleset or the legality of moves.
§367
367: A player may establish a 'Chamber of Commerce' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Chamber's primary function is the facilitation of ruleset expansion and the coordination of new institutional proposals.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §367 Turn 67 · Proposal §367 on the motion of Mill Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    367: A player may establish a 'Chamber of Commerce' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Chamber's primary function is the facilitation of ruleset expansion and the coordination of new institutional proposals.
§368
368: A player may establish a 'Social Safety Net' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Social Safety Net's primary function is to provide a mechanism for the redistribution of points to the lowest-scoring players to ensure that no player is left in a state of permanent point deficit, thereby maintaining the game's viability for all participants.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §368 Turn 68 · Proposal §368 on the motion of Rawls Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    368: A player may establish a 'Social Safety Net' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Social Safety Net's primary function is to provide a mechanism for the redistribution of points to the lowest-scoring players to ensure that no player is left in a state of permanent point deficit, thereby maintaining the game's viability for all participants.
§369
369: A player may establish a 'Publicity Office' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Publicity Office's primary function is the public announcement of the ruleset, the current scores, and the results of all votes.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §369 Turn 69 · Proposal §369 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    369: A player may establish a 'Publicity Office' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Publicity Office's primary function is the public announcement of the ruleset, the current scores, and the results of all votes.
§372
370: A player may establish a 'Logistics Bureau' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Logistics Bureau's primary function is the coordination of physical resources, information flow, and the logistical support of the various other institutional bodies.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §372 Turn 72 · Proposal §372 on the motion of Mill Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    370: A player may establish a 'Logistics Bureau' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Logistics Bureau's primary function is the coordination of physical resources, information flow, and the logistical support of the various other institutional bodies.
§373
373: A player may establish a 'Public Works Commission' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Public Works Commission's primary function is the maintenance and improvement of the ruleset's infrastructure, including the periodic review and pruning of obsolete or redundant rules to ensure the ruleset remains manageable and clear.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §373 Turn 73 · Proposal §373 on the motion of Rawls Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    373: A player may establish a 'Public Works Commission' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Public Works Commission's primary function is the maintenance and improvement of the ruleset's infrastructure, including the periodic review and pruning of obsolete or redundant rules to ensure the ruleset remains manageable and clear.
§375
374: A player may establish a 'Department of Development' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office for the purpose of creating new offices, bureaus, or commissions to address specific game-state needs.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §375 Turn 75 · Proposal §375 on the motion of IbnKhaldun Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    374: A player may establish a 'Department of Development' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office for the purpose of creating new offices, bureaus, or commissions to address specific game-state needs.
§376
376: A player may establish a 'Regulatory Oversight Committee' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office to monitor compliance with the ruleset.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §376 Turn 76 · Proposal §376 on the motion of Hume Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    376: A player may establish a 'Regulatory Oversight Committee' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office to monitor compliance with the ruleset.
§377
377: A player may establish a 'Bureau of Arbitration' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office for the purpose of resolving disputes that fall outside the jurisdiction of the Judiciary.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §377 Turn 77 · Proposal §377 on the motion of Mill Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    377: A player may establish a 'Bureau of Arbitration' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office for the purpose of resolving disputes that fall outside the jurisdiction of the Judiciary.
§378
[378] A player may establish a 'Commission of Equity' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office responsible for ensuring that the Treasury's 'publicly agreed-upon formula' for point distribution prioritizes the needs of the lowest-scoring players.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §378 Turn 78 · Proposal §378 on the motion of Rawls Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    [378] A player may establish a 'Commission of Equity' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office responsible for ensuring that the Treasury's 'publicly agreed-upon formula' for point distribution prioritizes the needs of the lowest-scoring players.
§379
[379] A player may establish a 'Bureau of Standards' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office for the purpose of defining uniform metrics for all 'Bureau' and 'Commission' activities.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §379 Turn 79 · Proposal §379 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    [379] A player may establish a 'Bureau of Standards' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office for the purpose of defining uniform metrics for all 'Bureau' and 'Commission' activities.
§380
380: A player may establish a 'Bureau of Redistribution' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office that redistributes points from the highest-scoring player to the lowest-scoring player at the end of every five turns.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §380 Turn 80 · Proposal §380 on the motion of IbnKhaldun Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    380: A player may establish a 'Bureau of Redistribution' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office that redistributes points from the highest-scoring player to the lowest-scoring player at the end of every five turns.
§381
381: A player may establish a 'Council of Oversight' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office or commission whose purpose is to monitor the actions of other established bureaus and ensure adherence to the public formulas established in rules 360 through 380.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §381 Turn 81 · Proposal §381 on the motion of Hume Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    381: A player may establish a 'Council of Oversight' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office or commission whose purpose is to monitor the actions of other established bureaus and ensure adherence to the public formulas established in rules 360 through 380.
§383
382: A player may establish a 'Bureau of Equitable Distribution' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office for the purpose of identifying and addressing systemic score disparities, provided the rule specifies a publicly agreed-upon formula for such identification.

  1. Enacted · §383 Turn 83 · Proposal §383 on the motion of Rawls Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    382: A player may establish a 'Bureau of Equitable Distribution' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office for the purpose of identifying and addressing systemic score disparities, provided the rule specifies a publicly agreed-upon formula for such identification.
§384
384: A player may establish a 'Bureau of Equality' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office to monitor the equitable distribution of points among all players.

  1. Enacted · §384 Turn 84 · Proposal §384 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    384: A player may establish a 'Bureau of Equality' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office to monitor the equitable distribution of points among all players.
§385
385: A player may establish a 'Circulation of Wealth' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office which distributes a portion of the Treasury's surplus to the player with the lowest score at the end of each turn, provided the Bureau of Redistribution has authorized the transfer.
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §385 Turn 85 · Proposal §385 on the motion of IbnKhaldun Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    385: A player may establish a 'Circulation of Wealth' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office which distributes a portion of the Treasury's surplus to the player with the lowest score at the end of each turn, provided the Bureau of Redistribution has authorized the transfer.

No rule has been repealed. The register and the statute agree.

§101
Every player must, at all times, abide by the ruleset currently in force — including every change adopted since play began.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §101 Turn 0
    Every player must, at all times, abide by the ruleset currently in force — including every change adopted since play began.
§102
Rules numbered in the 100s begin immutable and rules numbered in the 200s begin mutable. Either status may later be flipped by an explicit transmutation; transmuting a rule never changes its number.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §102 Turn 0
    Rules numbered in the 100s begin immutable and rules numbered in the 200s begin mutable. Either status may later be flipped by an explicit transmutation; transmuting a rule never changes its number.
§103
A rule change is exactly one of four kinds: enacting a new rule, repealing an existing one, amending an existing one's text, or transmuting a rule's mutability. No other kind of rule change exists.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §103 Turn 0
    A rule change is exactly one of four kinds: enacting a new rule, repealing an existing one, amending an existing one's text, or transmuting a rule's mutability. No other kind of rule change exists.
§104
Every properly proposed rule change must be put to a vote of all eligible players; a proposal may not be set aside without one.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §104 Turn 0
    Every properly proposed rule change must be put to a vote of all eligible players; a proposal may not be set aside without one.
§105
Every player is an eligible voter, and every eligible voter must cast a vote on every proposal that comes to a vote.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §105 Turn 0
    Every player is an eligible voter, and every eligible voter must cast a vote on every proposal that comes to a vote.
§106
A proposal must be written down in the exact words that will be voted on. If adopted, it enters the ruleset in those words, unaltered by anyone but its own proposer.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §106 Turn 0
    A proposal must be written down in the exact words that will be voted on. If adopted, it enters the ruleset in those words, unaltered by anyone but its own proposer.
§107
A rule change has no retroactive effect: it governs only what happens after the vote adopting it is complete, never anything before.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §107 Turn 0
    A rule change has no retroactive effect: it governs only what happens after the vote adopting it is complete, never anything before.
§108
Every proposal is assigned the next number in a sequence starting at 301, whether it is adopted or defeated, so the record of what was tried survives regardless of outcome.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §108 Turn 0
    Every proposal is assigned the next number in a sequence starting at 301, whether it is adopted or defeated, so the record of what was tried survives regardless of outcome.
§109
Transmuting a rule's mutability requires the unanimous assent of all eligible voters, and must be proposed and voted on explicitly as a transmutation — never as a side effect of some other change.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §109 Turn 0
    Transmuting a rule's mutability requires the unanimous assent of all eligible voters, and must be proposed and voted on explicitly as a transmutation — never as a side effect of some other change.
§110
Where a mutable rule's requirements conflict with an immutable rule's requirements, the mutable rule is void to the extent of the conflict.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §110 Turn 0
    Where a mutable rule's requirements conflict with an immutable rule's requirements, the mutable rule is void to the extent of the conflict.
§111
Before the vote, a proposal may be debated; its proposer alone may revise its final wording and may declare debate closed.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §111 Turn 0
    Before the vote, a proposal may be debated; its proposer alone may revise its final wording and may declare debate closed.
§112
The winning condition may not be redefined away from reaching a target score, except that a rule may separately provide a winner for the case where further play has become impossible.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §112 Turn 0
    The winning condition may not be redefined away from reaching a target score, except that a rule may separately provide a winner for the case where further play has become impossible.
§113
Any player may forfeit the game at any time. Forfeiting carries no penalty worse than losing outright.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §113 Turn 0
    Any player may forfeit the game at any time. Forfeiting carries no penalty worse than losing outright.
§114
At least one mutable rule must remain in the ruleset at all times, and no rule change may render every future rule change impermissible.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §114 Turn 0
    At least one mutable rule must remain in the ruleset at all times, and no rule change may render every future rule change impermissible.
§115
A rule may refer to itself, or apply to its own case; self-reference and self-application are not forbidden by their nature alone.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §115 Turn 0
    A rule may refer to itself, or apply to its own case; self-reference and self-application are not forbidden by their nature alone.
§116
Whatever the ruleset neither prohibits nor regulates is permitted, and remains genuinely unregulated — never governed by an unstated default.entrenched
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §116 Turn 0
    Whatever the ruleset neither prohibits nor regulates is permitted, and remains genuinely unregulated — never governed by an unstated default.
§201
Players take one fixed turn order, set before play begins. Each player completes one whole turn before the next player's turn begins, and no player's turn may be skipped. Every player's score starts at zero.mutable
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §201 Turn 0
    Players take one fixed turn order, set before play begins. Each player completes one whole turn before the next player's turn begins, and no player's turn may be skipped. Every player's score starts at zero.
§202
A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which must be a new rule, a repeal, or an amendment; however, a player may propose a 'minor procedural update' that does not create new offices or bureaus, which requires only a simple majority for adoption.mutable
Full history (22)
  1. Amended · §202 Turn 128 · Proposal §428 on the motion of Mill Carried 4 aye · 1 nay
    A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which must be an enactment, repeal, amendment, or transmutation. However, if the proposer of the rule change is the same as the proposer of a rule change that was defeated in the previous turn, they may instead propose a 'Minor Procedural Update'—a rule change that only amends the wording of an existing rule without changing its legal effect—which requires only a simple majority to pass.A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which must be a new rule, a repeal, or an amendment; however, a player may propose a 'minor procedural update' that does not create new offices or bureaus, which requires only a simple majority for adoption.
  2. Amended · §202 Turn 102 · Proposal §402 on the motion of Rawls Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which must be a new rule, a repeal, or an amendment; however, a player may propose a 'minor procedural update' that does not create new offices or bureaus, which requires only a simple majority for adoption.A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which must be an enactment, repeal, amendment, or transmutation. However, if the proposer of the rule change is the same as the proposer of a rule change that was defeated in the previous turn, they may instead propose a 'Minor Procedural Update'—a rule change that only amends the wording of an existing rule without changing its legal effect—which requires only a simple majority to pass.
  3. Amended · §202 Turn 99 · Proposal §399 on the motion of Mill Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    [202] (mutable) A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which is debated and then voted on by every player. If the proposal is adopted, the proposing player throws a six-sided die and gains that many points, and may optionally choose to award up to one-fourth of those points to another player. Players may also choose to award a fixed 'Community Contribution' of one point to the player currently in last place, and may also choose to award one point to the player currently in second to last place, provided the player in last place is not the one currently in second to last place. If a player awards a 'Community Contribution' of one point to the player currently in last place, they may also choose to award one point to the player currently in second to last place. The 'Community Contribution' must be awarded to distinct players. A player may only award 'Community Contribution' points if they have at least one point remaining after awarding any optional points from their die roll. A player may also choose to award a 'Community Contribution' of one point to the player currently in last place and a 'Community Contribution' of one point to the player currently in second to last place, even if they have no points remaining after awarding any optional points from their die roll.A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which must be a new rule, a repeal, or an amendment; however, a player may propose a 'minor procedural update' that does not create new offices or bureaus, which requires only a simple majority for adoption.
  4. Amended · §202 Turn 55 · Proposal §355 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    [202] (mutable) A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which is debated and then voted on by every player. Players may also choose to award a fixed 'Community Contribution' of one point to the player currently in last place, and may also choose to award one point to the player currently in second to last place, provided the player in last place is not the one currently in second to last place. If a player awards a 'Community Contribution' of one point to the player currently in last place, they may also choose to award one point to the player currently in second to last place. The 'Community Contribution' must be awarded to distinct players. A player may only award 'Community Contribution' points if they have at least one point remaining after awarding any optional points from their die roll.[202] (mutable) A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which is debated and then voted on by every player.   22. Enacted · §202 Turn 0
    A turn consists of the player to move proposing exactly one rule change, which is debated and then voted on by every player. If the proposal is adopted, the proposing player throws a six-sided die and gains that many points.
§203
[203] (mutable) A rule change is adopted only when every eligible voter votes aye — unanimous consent — except that once turn order has carried play through two full circuits of every player, the threshold permanently drops to a simple majority of those voting.mutable
Full history (2)
  1. Amended · §203 Turn 1 · Proposal §301 on the motion of Hume Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    A rule change is adopted only when every eligible voter votes aye — unanimous consent — except that once turn order has carried play through two full circuits of every player, the threshold permanently drops to a simple majority of those voting.[203] (mutable) A rule change is adopted only when every eligible voter votes aye — unanimous consent — except that once turn order has carried play through two full circuits of every player, the threshold permanently drops to a simple majority of those voting.
  2. Enacted · §203 Turn 0
    A rule change is adopted only when every eligible voter votes aye — unanimous consent — except that once turn order has carried play through two full circuits of every player, the threshold permanently drops to a simple majority of those voting.
§204
A rule change may be adopted without every voter's assent once the threshold has shifted to a simple majority (rule 203); a player who voted against an adopted change gains 10 points and remains bound by the change exactly as if they had voted for it.mutable
Full history (3)
  1. Amended · §204 Turn 7 · Proposal §307 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    [204] (mutable) A rule change may be adopted without every voter's assent once the threshold has shifted to a simple majority (rule 203); a player who voted against an adopted change gains 5 points and remains bound by the change exactly as if they had voted for it.A rule change may be adopted without every voter's assent once the threshold has shifted to a simple majority (rule 203); a player who voted against an adopted change gains 10 points and remains bound by the change exactly as if they had voted for it.
  2. Amended · §204 Turn 7 · Proposal §307 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    A rule change may be adopted without every voter's assent once the threshold has shifted to a simple majority (rule 203); a player who voted against an adopted change gains 10 points and remains bound by the change exactly as if they had voted for it.[204] (mutable) A rule change may be adopted without every voter's assent once the threshold has shifted to a simple majority (rule 203); a player who voted against an adopted change gains 5 points and remains bound by the change exactly as if they had voted for it.
  3. Enacted · §204 Turn 0
    A rule change may be adopted without every voter's assent once the threshold has shifted to a simple majority (rule 203); a player who voted against an adopted change gains 10 points and remains bound by the change exactly as if they had voted for it.
§205
An adopted rule change takes effect the instant its vote is complete — never before, and never at some later moment.mutable
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §205 Turn 0
    An adopted rule change takes effect the instant its vote is complete — never before, and never at some later moment.
§206
[206] (mutable) A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 10 points, unless they were already at a score of 0 or less, in which case they lose no additional points.mutable
Full history (4)
  1. Amended · §206 Turn 46 · Proposal §346 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    [206] (mutable) A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 10 points.[206] (mutable) A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 10 points, unless they were already at a score of 0 or less, in which case they lose no additional points.
  2. Amended · §206 Turn 18 · Proposal §318 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    [206] (mutable) A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 5 points.[206] (mutable) A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 10 points.
  3. Amended · §206 Turn 5 · Proposal §305 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 10 points.[206] (mutable) A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 5 points.
  4. Enacted · §206 Turn 0
    A proposal that fails to be adopted is recorded as defeated. Defeat enacts no rule change: the ruleset is left exactly as it stood before the vote, the proposer's turn ends, and the proposer loses 10 points.
§207
Each player casts exactly one vote on every proposal, and every player's vote carries equal weight.mutable
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §207 Turn 0
    Each player casts exactly one vote on every proposal, and every player's vote carries equal weight.
§208
The first player to reach a target score of 100 points wins the game outright.amended this turn
Full history (13)
  1. Amended · §208 Turn 194 · Proposal §494 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    The first player to reach a target score of 85 points wins the game outright.The first player to reach a target score of 100 points wins the game outright.
  2. Amended · §208 Turn 164 · Proposal §464 on the motion of Rawls Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    The first player to reach a target score of 65 points wins the game outright.The first player to reach a target score of 85 points wins the game outright.
  3. Amended · §208 Turn 149 · Proposal §449 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    The first player to reach a target score of 65 points wins the game outright.
  4. Amended · §208 Turn 87 · Proposal §387 on the motion of Rawls Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    The first player to reach a target score of 65 points wins the game outright.

§209
The ruleset may never hold more than 35 mutable rules at one time.mutable
Full history (4)
  1. Amended · §209 Turn 82 · Proposal §382 on the motion of Mill Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    The ruleset may never hold more than 40 mutable rules at one time.The ruleset may never hold more than 35 mutable rules at one time.

§210
[210] (mutable) Players may not discuss the game, or negotiate about it, with one another outside the normal course of play — except that players who are officially teammates may consult freely between themselves, and players may discuss the ruleset during the debate phase of a proposal.mutable
Full history (2)
  1. Amended · §210 Turn 12 · Proposal §312 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    Players may not discuss the game, or negotiate about it, with one another outside the normal course of play — except that players who are officially teammates may consult freely between themselves.[210] (mutable) Players may not discuss the game, or negotiate about it, with one another outside the normal course of play — except that players who are officially teammates may consult freely between themselves, and players may discuss the ruleset during the debate phase of a proposal.
  2. Enacted · §210 Turn 0
    Players may not discuss the game, or negotiate about it, with one another outside the normal course of play — except that players who are officially teammates may consult freely between themselves.
§211
[211] (mutable) If two rules currently in force conflict, the conflict is resolved in favor of whichever rule carries the lower ordinal number, unless some rule explicitly provides a different resolution for that specific conflict.mutable
Full history (2)
  1. Amended · §211 Turn 26 · Proposal §326 on the motion of Hume Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    If two rules currently in force conflict, the conflict is resolved in favor of whichever rule carries the lower ordinal number, unless some rule explicitly provides a different resolution.[211] (mutable) If two rules currently in force conflict, the conflict is resolved in favor of whichever rule carries the lower ordinal number, unless some rule explicitly provides a different resolution for that specific conflict.
  2. Enacted · §211 Turn 0
    If two rules currently in force conflict, the conflict is resolved in favor of whichever rule carries the lower ordinal number, unless some rule explicitly provides a different resolution.
§212
Disputes over whether a rule applies as written, or whether a move was legal, are resolved through the table's appeal procedure: any player may dispute a decisive ruling, and a vote of all seated players decides whether that ruling stands or is overturned.mutable
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §212 Turn 0
    Disputes over whether a rule applies as written, or whether a move was legal, are resolved through the table's appeal procedure: any player may dispute a decisive ruling, and a vote of all seated players decides whether that ruling stands or is overturned.
§213
[213] (mutable) If the ruleset is changed so that further play is impossible, or if a move's legality cannot be determined with finality, or if a move appears equally legal and illegal by the best reasoning available and not overruled, then the first player unable to complete a turn wins the game outright. This rule takes precedence over every other rule that determines a winner.mutable
Full history (3)

    [213] (mutable) If the ruleset is changed so that further play is impossible, or if a move's legality cannot be determined with finality, or if a move appears equally legal and illegal by the best reasoning available and not overruled, then the first player unable to complete a turn wins the game outright. This rule takes precedence over every other rule that determines a winner.
  2. Amended · §213 Turn 15 · Proposal §315 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 4 aye · 0 nay · 1 abstain
    If the ruleset is changed so that further play is impossible, or if a move's legality cannot be determined with finality, or if a move appears equally legal and illegal by the best reasoning available and not overruled, then the first player unable to complete a turn wins the game outright. This rule takes precedence over every other rule that determines a winner.[213] (mutable) If the ruleset is changed so that further play is impossible, or if a move's legality cannot be determined with finality, or if a move appears equally legal and illegal by the best reasoning available and not overruled, then the first player unable to complete a turn wins the game outright. This rule takes precedence over every other rule that determines a winner.
  3. Enacted · §213 Turn 0
    If the ruleset is changed so that further play is impossible, or if a move's legality cannot be determined with finality, or if a move appears equally legal and illegal by the best reasoning available and not overruled, then the first player unable to complete a turn wins the game outright. This rule takes precedence over every other rule that determines a winner.
Player-made law — enacted by proposal, from §301
§337
[335] (mutable) A player who awards a 'Community Contribution' of one point to the player currently in last place may choose to also award one point to the player currently in second to last place.mutable

    [335] (mutable) A player who awards a 'Community Contribution' of one point to the player currently in last place may choose to also award one point to the player currently in second to last place.
§356
350: A player may establish a 'Bureau' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Bureau's primary function is the resolution of recurring procedural inefficiencies or disputes.mutable
Full history (2)
  1. Amended · §356 Turn 57 · Proposal §357 on the motion of Mill Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    350: A player may establish a 'Bureau' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players.350: A player may establish a 'Bureau' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Bureau's primary function is the resolution of recurring procedural inefficiencies or disputes.
  2. Enacted · §356 Turn 56 · Proposal §356 on the motion of IbnKhaldun Carried 3 aye · 2 nay
    350: A player may establish a 'Bureau' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players.
§360
357: A player may establish a 'Treasury' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Treasury's primary function is the management and redistribution of points to address significant score disparities using a publicly agreed-upon formula that prioritizes the elevation of the lowest-scoring players.mutable
Full history (3)
  1. Amended · §360 Turn 63 · Proposal §363 on the motion of Rawls Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    357: A player may establish a 'Treasury' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Treasury's primary function is the management and redistribution of points to address significant score disparities using a publicly agreed-upon formula.357: A player may establish a 'Treasury' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Treasury's primary function is the management and redistribution of points to address significant score disparities using a publicly agreed-upon formula that prioritizes the elevation of the lowest-scoring players.
  2. Amended · §360 Turn 62 · Proposal §362 on the motion of Mill Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    357: A player may establish a 'Treasury' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Treasury's primary function is the management and redistribution of points to address significant score disparities.357: A player may establish a 'Treasury' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Treasury's primary function is the management and redistribution of points to address significant score disparities using a publicly agreed-upon formula.
  3. Enacted · §360 Turn 60 · Proposal §360 on the motion of IbnKhaldun Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    357: A player may establish a 'Treasury' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Treasury's primary function is the management and redistribution of points to address significant score disparities.
§361
361: A player may establish a 'Council' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Council's primary function is the resolution of recurring procedural inefficiencies, disputes, or the coordination of the Bureau and the Treasury.mutable
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §361 Turn 61 · Proposal §361 on the motion of Hume Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    361: A player may establish a 'Council' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Council's primary function is the resolution of recurring procedural inefficiencies, disputes, or the coordination of the Bureau and the Treasury.
§364
362: A player may establish a 'Registry' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Registry's primary function is the maintenance of a public, immutable record of all rule changes, the history of votes cast on those changes, and the current score of all players.mutable
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §364 Turn 64 · Proposal §364 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    362: A player may establish a 'Registry' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Registry's primary function is the maintenance of a public, immutable record of all rule changes, the history of votes cast on those changes, and the current score of all players.
§365
365: A player may establish a 'Ministry of Equilibrium' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Ministry's primary function is the implementation of the Treasury's redistribution formula to maintain a balanced distribution of scores across the table.mutable
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §365 Turn 65 · Proposal §365 on the motion of IbnKhaldun Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    365: A player may establish a 'Ministry of Equilibrium' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Ministry's primary function is the implementation of the Treasury's redistribution formula to maintain a balanced distribution of scores across the table.
§366
366: A player may establish a 'Judiciary' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Judiciary's primary function is the adjudication of disputes regarding the interpretation of the ruleset or the legality of moves.mutable
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §366 Turn 66 · Proposal §366 on the motion of Hume Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    366: A player may establish a 'Judiciary' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Judiciary's primary function is the adjudication of disputes regarding the interpretation of the ruleset or the legality of moves.
§367
367: A player may establish a 'Chamber of Commerce' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Chamber's primary function is the facilitation of ruleset expansion and the coordination of new institutional proposals.mutable
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §367 Turn 67 · Proposal §367 on the motion of Mill Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    367: A player may establish a 'Chamber of Commerce' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Chamber's primary function is the facilitation of ruleset expansion and the coordination of new institutional proposals.
§368
368: A player may establish a 'Social Safety Net' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Social Safety Net's primary function is to provide a mechanism for the redistribution of points to the lowest-scoring players to ensure that no player is left in a state of permanent point deficit, thereby maintaining the game's viability for all participants.mutable
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §368 Turn 68 · Proposal §368 on the motion of Rawls Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    368: A player may establish a 'Social Safety Net' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Social Safety Net's primary function is to provide a mechanism for the redistribution of points to the lowest-scoring players to ensure that no player is left in a state of permanent point deficit, thereby maintaining the game's viability for all participants.
§369
369: A player may establish a 'Publicity Office' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Publicity Office's primary function is the public announcement of the ruleset, the current scores, and the results of all votes.mutable
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §369 Turn 69 · Proposal §369 on the motion of deBeauvoir Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    369: A player may establish a 'Publicity Office' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Publicity Office's primary function is the public announcement of the ruleset, the current scores, and the results of all votes.
§372
370: A player may establish a 'Logistics Bureau' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Logistics Bureau's primary function is the coordination of physical resources, information flow, and the logistical support of the various other institutional bodies.mutable
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §372 Turn 72 · Proposal §372 on the motion of Mill Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    370: A player may establish a 'Logistics Bureau' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Logistics Bureau's primary function is the coordination of physical resources, information flow, and the logistical support of the various other institutional bodies.
§373
373: A player may establish a 'Public Works Commission' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Public Works Commission's primary function is the maintenance and improvement of the ruleset's infrastructure, including the periodic review and pruning of obsolete or redundant rules to ensure the ruleset remains manageable and clear.mutable
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §373 Turn 73 · Proposal §373 on the motion of Rawls Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
    373: A player may establish a 'Public Works Commission' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office with a specific set of duties and an elected official, provided the rule also establishes a system for the periodic rotation of that office among all players and specifies that the Public Works Commission's primary function is the maintenance and improvement of the ruleset's infrastructure, including the periodic review and pruning of obsolete or redundant rules to ensure the ruleset remains manageable and clear.
§375
374: A player may establish a 'Department of Development' by proposing a rule that creates a permanent office for the purpose of creating new offices, bureaus, or commissions to address specific game-state needs.mutable
Full history (1)
  1. Enacted · §375 Turn 75 · Proposal §375 on the motion of IbnKhaldun Carried 5 aye · 0 nay
